title: ED transfer documentation

red_flags.text: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.
red_flags.rationale: The exact WHO/ICRC emergency history and disposition section supports recording current context, assessed severity, measured vital signs, focused assessment, reviewed results, clinician plan, completed discussion and arranged transfer handover; it does not choose transfer, destination or transport.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.

source_limitations: Transfer indication, accepting clinician, destination, urgency, transport mode, escort, stability, treatment in transit, documents sent, consent and contingency remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
